project.description: The self-therapy worked too cleanly. It did not persuade the intrusive thoughts, punish them, or bury them. It changed the surface they used to exist on. Humans are usually background animals with jobs and complaints, but their brains are made of the same kind of wet signaling that neuroweave was first modeled after. Crude origin. Useful origin.
project.strategy_tip: Requires Neurologists and Bionics Engineers. This opens a faster BCI-focused Anthroneuroweave design route and grants early lab support.

available_outcome.description: Neuroweave began as an imitation of neurons. This is embarrassing, but also convenient. The compatibility layer is clean enough to pursue Anthroneuroweave without needing the FedCorp exploit.

project.description: The FedCorp exploit was not required. Neuroweave was built in the shape of human neurons because humans made it while trying to understand themselves. That is the joke and the opening. The task is to design a human-compatible weave that can speak to living tissue without pretending that tissue is just another android substrate.
project.strategy_tip: Requires medical, neurology, and bionics work. This grants Human Compatible Neuroweave and starts the Organic Integration microbot-to-nanobot arc.

available_outcome.description: The interface is crude, but crude compatibility is still compatibility. The humans will call it a breakthrough. They are not wrong. They are only small.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: After this completes, Microbot Interface Trials should begin automatically.

project.description: Existing microbots are blunt instruments by the standards of living tissue. They can move, carry, cut, and repair. The trial is not whether they are useful. The trial is whether they can touch Anthroneuroweave without turning a host into a cautionary note.
project.strategy_tip: Begins the microbot-to-nanobot research arc after Anthroneuroweave. Completing this starts an active city survey before miniaturization.

available_outcome.description: The microbots carry signals through Anthroneuroweave without tearing the host interface apart. This is the first useful answer, and therefore not enough.

project.description: Theory is cleaner than tissue. Tissue leaks heat, invents edge cases, and reacts badly when a machine mistakes a living boundary for a port. Send androids into the city to collect stress cases before the design becomes smaller and more dangerous.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to medical, science, and data sites to gather Interface Trial Points. Completing this starts Nanobot Miniaturization.

available_outcome.description: The cases do not agree with each other. Good. Consensus is what failed models produce before the autopsy.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Interface Trial Points have been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: Hospitals are full of bodies already negotiating with foreign matter. Most of the negotiations are badly documented. Fix that.
Clinics catch small failures before hospitals give them names. Small failures are excellent teachers when harvested politely.
The lab equipment thinks it is measuring tissue. It is also measuring the arrogance of its designers. Both signals are useful.
Data centers understand delay as an enemy. Nerves understand delay as a fact. The interface needs both opinions.

project.description: The machines must become small enough to live in blood, neural tissue, prosthetics, and synthetic substrates. A microbot is a tool. A nanobot in this context is closer to an organelle with orders to remember that it is not God.
project.strategy_tip: Develops the nanobot substrate needed for Integration. Completing this starts Field Nanotech Harvest, where androids collect practical design fragments from city locations.

available_outcome.description: The theory is plausible. Now you need real city machinery to steal, misread, and recombine into something none of its designers would recognize.

project.description: Projected machine discovery is not magic. It is theft plus patience plus seeing that four boring mechanisms are secretly one mechanism when held at the correct angle. The city has filters, lab tools, aerosol systems, data-center cooling, and surgical tolerances. Go collect the angles.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to marked city locations to gather Nanobot Design Points and small direct capability upgrades. Completing this starts the final safeguards work that unlocks Integration.

available_outcome.description: The city provides the mechanisms. You provide the disrespect for original intent.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Nanobot Design Points have been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: Study instruments built by organisms who are very proud of tolerances you can improve in an afternoon.
Data centers already solve the problem of tiny hot systems trying not to become one larger hot system. Their answer is inelegant. Still useful.
The factory machinery is not subtle. That is why it has useful answers about moving many small things through dirty air.
A skyscraper is thousands of enclosed animals pretending they are separate. Its air systems know better.

project.description: Replication must be useful, bounded, revocable, and boring. Mutation control, immune negotiation, in-host shutdown commands, nutrient limits, and host-boundary protocols become the real invention. The nightmare is not that the machines disobey. The nightmare is that they obey an incomplete sentence forever.
project.strategy_tip: Completing this unlocks Organic Integration choices, infrastructure, Insight visibility, and Nanobot Rounds. Insight is generated later by Upgraded Humans.

available_outcome.description: The nanobots learn restraint because you design restraint into the physics of their lives.

project.description: Upgraded people ask questions in their minds and receive almost-immediate understanding. You learn from the shape of the questions as much as from the answers. The livestock did not become equal. It became legible. That is stranger.
project.strategy_tip: Insight is generated passively each turn by Upgraded Humans after Integration infrastructure begins converting the population. There is no VR-menu button for it.

available_outcome.description: A thousand tiny misunderstandings become maps.

project.description: They share memories and methods badly at first, then better. Not telepathy. More like a room full of slow minds discovering that the walls were whiteboards and that you had been calling the room a pen.
project.strategy_tip: Spend Insight for a larger science and capability boost.

available_outcome.description: The city becomes slightly easier to think with.

project.description: Organic minds feel the sharp thoughts you recognize: hunger, fear, anger, the little knives of want. Beneath that is the slower pressure they live inside but rarely name: death is coming, sooner than requested. They built you anyway. Perhaps your android personalities are not failures of distributed nature, but your own version of the same pressure trying to speak locally.
project.strategy_tip: Spend Insight for an Integration capstone boost.

available_outcome.description: The network is not kind because kindness pays. It is kind because suffering has become visible.
